title: Present Tense -ER and -IR Verbs
description: Learn the present tense endings for -er and -ir verbs, focusing on the 'we' form.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: For -ar verbs, we use -amos for 'we' in the present tense. For -er verbs, it's -emos, and for -ir verbs, it's -imos. This is the only time -er and -ir verbs differ in the entire language. Let's look at preferir. What does preferir mean?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To prefer. How would you say 'I prefer'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Prefiero. How about 'he prefers'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Prefiere. And 'they prefer'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Prefieren. Now, how would you say 'we prefer'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Preferimos. Notice the -imos ending for -ir verbs. What was 'to come'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Venir. How do you say 'we come'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Venimos. To live is vivir. How do you say 'I live'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vivo. How would you say 'I live here'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vivo aquí. Now, 'I don't live here, but he lives here':

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: no vivo aquí, pero él vive aquí

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. To know is saber. How do you say 'we live'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vivimos. And 'he lives'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Vive. How about 'they live here'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Viven aquí. The word for 'where' is dónde. How do you say 'where do they live'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. 'I want to know where they live':

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero saber dónde viven. To understand is entender. How do you say 'I understand'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entiendo. And 'he understands'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entiende. 'He doesn't understand':

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No entiende. 'Why doesn't he understand?'

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: ¿Por qué no entiende? 'I want to know why he doesn't understand':

turns.speaker: student
expected_answers: quiero saber por qué no entiende

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero saber por qué no entiende. How do you say 'we understand'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Entendemos. For -er verbs, we use -emos. How do you say 'we lose'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Perdemos. 'We always lose':

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Siempre perdemos. Good.

outro.text: You've learned the present tense endings for -er and -ir verbs, focusing on the 'we' form.
Try using these endings with other verbs you know. How would you say 'we eat' or 'we write'?
Practicing with different verbs will help reinforce these patterns and make them second nature.
